Request via AWS CLI to 'Start Instance' & 'RequestServiceQuotaIncrease'

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API / Exposed Credentials in Public Storage
attack_chain.description: Attacker discovered exposed IAM access keys stored in a public S3 bucket, providing the initial foothold into the AWS environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Exposed IAM Access keys in Public S3

attack_chain.description: Attacker authenticated to AWS using the compromised IAM access keys via the AWS CLI, gaining initial access as a legitimate user.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Request via AWS CLI to 'Start Instance' & 'RequestServiceQuotaIncrease'

attack_chain.description: Attacker issued CLI commands to start EC2 instances and request service quota increases, probing the environment's capabilities and limits.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Request via AWS CLI to 'Start Instance' & 'RequestServiceQuotaIncrease'

attack_chain.description: Via CLI, attacker created new IAM resources (users/roles), assigned additional permissions, and deployed a new microservice. SES and SNS quotas and sending rates were also increased.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Via CLI New IAM Resource created, assigned additional permissions & New microservice deployed. Increase Sending Quote initiated & Sending Rate, and SNS quota

attack_chain.description: Two additional new IAM resources were created and assigned elevated permissions and roles specifically for SES and SQS access using AttachUserPolicy.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Two new IAM Resource created & assigned additional permissions and roles for SES & SQS (AttachUserPolicy) SES commands issued to verify new domain, DKIM and email address

attack_chain.description: Attacker laterally moved by pivoting to SES and SQS services using the newly created IAM users/roles, issuing SES commands to verify a new domain, configure DKIM, and verify email addresses for the phishing campaign.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: SES commands issued to verify new domain, DKIM and email address

attack_chain.description: Attacker deleted profile elements, policies, and access keys for a previously created IAM user to cover tracks. This activity was executed from an external IP address not belonging to the customer.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Delete profile elements, policies and access keys for a previously created user, executed from external IP not owned by customer

attack_chain.description: Attacker leveraged the hijacked SES infrastructure to send an abnormally large volume of emails as part of a mass phishing campaign, causing SES bounce rate issues that triggered an AWS notification to the customer.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Sending an abnormally large amount of emails in mass phishing campaign. Customer contacted by AWS re SES email bounce rate issue and rate increase

impact_type: Resource Hijacking / Mass Phishing via SES
initial_access_vector: Exposed IAM access keys discovered in a public S3 bucket, used via AWS CLI to authenticate into the environment
persistence_mechanism: Creation of multiple new IAM users/roles with AttachUserPolicy granting elevated permissions for SES, SQS, and SNS; new microservice deployed
privilege_escalation: AttachUserPolicy used to assign additional permissions and roles to newly created IAM resources, granting SES and SQS access beyond the original compromised account's scope
